creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
[I finally have access to the powerpc/powerpc64 machines, at least for a bit.]

powerpc64 gets the same db5_item_space_problem.c output as the little endian contexts do (compiled by the system clang 3.8.0):

# ./a.out
in f: i=-17 (as long: i=65519)
in f: j=-25536 (as long: j=40000)
in main: i=65519
in f: i=-25536 (as long: i=40000)
in f: j=-17 (as long: j=65519)
in main: i=40000
in f: i=0 (as long: i=0)
in f: j=0 (as long: j=0)
in main: i=0

This was on:

# uname -apKU
FreeBSD FBSDG5C0 11.0-ALPHA5 FreeBSD 11.0-ALPHA5 #40 r302214M: Tue Oct 18 06:11:02 PDT 2016     root@FBSDG5C0:/usr/obj/xtoolchain/powerpc.powerpc64/usr/src/sys/GENERIC64vtsc-NODEBUG  powerpc powerpc64 1100120 1100120

gcc6 matches for its results.

So the behavior happens to be invariant to little vs. big endian for the promoted types not matching the prototypes' types in that example code.

Thus for use of item_space and item_size in db5: if 'indx + O_ONDX' happens to overflow for one of the tested contexts the results should be the same (despite any variations in little vs. big endian).
